behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: Escalation phrase: "embedding vector produced ... has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index" requires validating the real embedding model output contract. A lower System/Integration test with mocked vectors can miss a real failure where the provider model/version returns 3072 dims while the index expects 1536, causing runtime rejection only in production. Rejected higher level Workflow: behavior checks a single model-output contract, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding endpoint with a fixed query and assert returned vector length exactly matches the live index dimension before insertion/search; fail on any mismatch.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: Escalation phrase: "similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score" targets real vector DB ranking behavior. A lower Integration test using an in-process fake can miss concrete failures such as ANN index configuration/metric mismatch (e.g., dot-product index with cosine expectation) that returns non-monotonic scores only in the real DB engine. Rejected higher level Agentic: this behavior is about DB ordering semantics, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a real vector database index with controlled vectors, execute search through the API stack, and assert returned scores are monotonically non-increasing and correspond to cosine ranking from the DB response.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: Escalation phrase: "LLM re-ranking ... changes the order ... when the query has nuanced intent" depends on real model interpretation quality. A lower Integration test with stubbed reranker output would always pass and miss a real failure where prompt/model changes stop intent-aware reordering and preserve raw ranking. Rejected higher level Workflow: this behavior validates one agentic decision point (rerank), not a multi-agentic chain requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Run retrieval on a curated nuanced-intent query set against the real reranking model; compare pre/post rank lists and assert at least one meaningful position change on expected cases.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: Escalation phrase: "no close matches ... returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise" depends on semantic embedding/relevance behavior, not only threshold code. A lower Integration test with fixed mocked similarities can miss a real failure where the embedding model maps out-of-domain queries near unrelated items, producing noisy non-empty results. Rejected higher level Workflow: behavior is a single decision outcome (empty vs noise), not multiple agentic handoffs that must be validated in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use real embedding + retrieval (and rerank path as configured) on out-of-domain/no-match queries in a controlled corpus and assert final results are empty under production relevance thresholds.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: Escalation phrase: "end-to-end latency ... to returning re-ranked results" spans multiple sequential AI-backed stages (embedding then LLM rerank) plus vector search and API transit. A lower Agentic/System test can miss a real failure where individually acceptable stage latencies compose to p50 >2s due orchestration overhead and cross-stage queuing in the full pipeline. Workflow is required because the measured property is the chained multi-step agentic path.
behaviors.test_description: Execute representative query traffic through the full production-like pipeline (real API, real vector DB, real embedding call, real rerank call), collect end-to-end timings, and assert median latency remains under 2.0s.
